query: DOJ Live Nation Ticketmaster March 2026 settlement terms divest amphitheater booking agreements service fee cap 15% exclusivity contract four years states object

mako.raw:
{"query":"DOJ Live Nation Ticketmaster March 2026 settlement terms divest amphitheater booking agreements service fee cap 15% exclusivity contract four years states object","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1633,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.justice.gov/atr/media/1443801/dl","title":null,"content":"�!Q endstream endobj startxref 437285 %%EOF 42 0 obj <>stream application/pdf Notice of Settlement: U.S. and Plaintiff States v."},{"url":"https://www.usatoday.com/story/entertainment/music/2026/03/09/live-nation-settlement/89063758007/","title":"Live Nation settles with DOJ – What it means for live entertainment fans","content":"DOJ settles with Live Nation requiring venue sales and fee limits as states split on the agreement.
Live Nation has agreed to settle with the department in a deal that includes the divestment of up to 13 of its amphitheaters nationwide and puts in place a 15% cap on service fees for people who want to use the venues, according to a senior Justice Department official.
Ticketmaster, a subsidiary of Live Nation, is also required to offer its technology to other ticket sellers, such as StubHub, to reach customers.
The company, an industry power player that reportedly earned $25 billion in revenue and $500 million in profit last year, controls roughly 70%-80% of major concert and live‑event ticket sales and owns a significant share of venues and concert promotions.
Brian Berry, executive director of the Ticket Policy Forum, who spoke at a Senate Commerce Committee hearing on live concert and event fees, called the deal a "token tap on the wrist.""},{"url":"https://newsroom.livenation.com/statements/live-nation-entertainment-reaches-settlement-with-u-s-department-of-justice/","title":"Live Nation Entertainment Reaches Settlement With U.S. Department of Justice – Live Nation Newsroom","content":"In addition, Live Nation will be divesting its 13 exclusive booking agreements with amphitheaters nationwide."},{"url":"https://www.ticketnews.com/2026/03/consumer-advocates-slam-reported-doj-live-nation-settlement-as-slap-on-the-wrist/","title":"Consumer Advocates, Policy Groups, and Lawmakers Slam Proposed Live Nation–Ticketmaster Settlement | TicketNews","content":"“The settlement reportedly requires Live Nation-Ticketmaster to commit to several conditions.
These include: a payment of $200 million in damages to the states; allowing rival ticketing companies to list tickets on the Ticketmaster platform; a 4-year limit on exclusive contracts with independent venues, with a carveout that allows them to use competitors for a portion of ticket sales; the divestiture of more than 10 Live Nation amphitheaters; and a cap on ticket service fees at its amphitheaters equal to 15% of face value."},{"url":"https://www.rollingstone.com/music/music-news/live-nation-settlement-need-to-know-next-1235528047/","title":"Live Nation Settelement: All You Need to Know and What Comes Next","content":"Well, along with needing judicial approval, Live Nation also needs to see if it can reach an agreement with all of the federal government’s co-plaintiffs: Attorneys general from nearly 40 states, plus Washington, D.C., who signed on to the suit when it was filed in 2024.
At a hearing Tuesday, March 10, Judge Arun Subramanian ordered the hold-out states to get back to the negotiating table with Live Nation and try to reach a deal by the end of this week.
“We support the terms of the settlement and welcome this resolution,” Jeff LeMaster, communications director for Arkansas AG Tim Griffin tells Rolling Stone via email.
Ticketmaster will also start capping its exclusivity contracts with venues at four years, and has agreed to offer concert venues the opportunity to choose either exclusive or non-exclusive arrangements.
Ticketmaster has agreed to cap service fees at 15 percent — but only at amphitheaters that Live Nation owns, operates, or controls.
During a background call with reporters, a senior justice department official said that Live Nation would be selling 13 different amphitheaters — and possibly more — further clarifying that there would be 13 assets available."}]}

Live Nation Entertainment, Inc and Ticketmaster ...","content":"v. Live Nation Entertainment, Inc., et al., No. 1:24-cv-3973-AS Dear Judge Subramanian: On behalf of the United States, attached is the settlement term sheet signed by the United States and by Defendants that was discussed in court today. ASSEFI Acting Assistant Attorney General Case 1:24-cv-03973-AS Document 1171-1 Filed 03/09/26 Page 1 of 7 Live Term Sheet for Resolution of United States et al. a. Ticketmaster will develop and implement a standardized API or other measures delivery of primary tickets that will for the listing, verification, authentication and provide the option to distribute primary tickets using any third-party primary marketplaces chosen by the venue, which will be made available to any venue, at the election of the venue, that utilizes Ticketmaster's back-office system ofrecord (\"TM Back-end\"). In addition to the ensure the measures described above can be used by venues under any existing contracts that remain in effect, Ticketmaster will provide venues the option to loosen exclusivity provisions in existing primary ticketing contracts that have years remaining as of the date of the entry of the decree.","score":0.75312227,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":2.75,"request_id":"dd9d8b81-b0e6-4901-9054-2297e8085696"}
